opportunities.name: National Lottery Community Fund - Awards for All England
opportunities.description: The largest community funder in the UK supporting projects that bring people together and improve communities. Funds work across four missions: communities come together, help children and young people thrive, enable healthier lives, and be environmentally sustainable.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The National Lottery Community Fund focuses on supporting people facing poverty, disadvantage or discrimination. They invest in community-led projects and want communities to shape decisions that affect their lives. They prioritize places and people experiencing the greatest need.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Projects up to 2 years bringing people together, supporting children and young people, enabling healthier lives, or environmental sustainability. Can fund salaries, volunteer expenses, building hire costs, training, equipment and running costs.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Projects that duplicate government statutory services. Work that doesn't align with their four missions. Organizations without proper governance.
opportunities.application_process: Applications can be submitted at any time through their online portal. Simple application form. Decision typically within 12-16 weeks for Awards for All. Must be at least 16 weeks before project starts.

opportunities.name: National Lottery Community Fund - Reaching Communities England
opportunities.description: Larger grants programme for organizations serving communities in England, supporting poverty reduction, disadvantage and discrimination. Projects must achieve one of the four NLCF missions and enable communities to shape decisions affecting their lives.
opportunities.funder_priorities: Focuses on places, people and communities experiencing poverty, disadvantage or discrimination. Supports community-led approaches where people shape decisions affecting their lives. Four missions: come together, environmentally sustainable, children and young people thrive, healthier lives.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Up to five years funding for community projects, capital costs, salaries, running costs, equipment. Statutory bodies including local authorities can apply. CICs, charities, CIOs, community benefit societies all eligible.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Services that are statutory responsibility of government. Projects that don't demonstrate community leadership or align with their missions.
opportunities.application_process: Two-stage application process. Takes on average 9 months to get funding. Stage one decision up to 4 months. Smaller amounts may be faster. Applications submitted at any time through online portal.

opportunities.name: Leathersellers' Main Charity Grants Programme
opportunities.description: Now in its fifth year, this programme aims to help prevent and address the harm of adverse childhood experiences (ACEs). Supports UK-registered charities that work with children, young people and parents affected by ACEs and mental ill-health.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Leathersellers' Company focuses on preventing and addressing adverse childhood experiences (ACEs). They care deeply about supporting children and young people recovering from trauma, and parents affected by ACEs and mental ill-health. Evidence-based interventions are valued.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Projects supporting children and young people recovering from ACEs, parents affected by ACEs and mental illness. Therapeutic interventions, trauma-informed support, early intervention projects. UK registered charities only.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations outside the UK. Projects not focused on ACEs or mental health in children and families. Individuals.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling application process. Apply through Leathersellers' Company website. Detailed application form required demonstrating understanding of ACEs and evidence-based approach.

opportunities.name: Skinners' Company - Young People Employment Grants (London & Kent)
opportunities.description: Funding available to registered charities and not-for-profit organisations in London and Kent to help young people from low-income backgrounds overcome poverty and disadvantage and move into employment.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Skinners' Company prioritizes helping young people from low-income backgrounds in London and Kent overcome poverty and disadvantage. They focus on employment pathways, skills training and removing barriers to work for disadvantaged youth.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Employment support programmes, vocational training, work readiness schemes, mentoring for employment, job coaching for young people from disadvantaged backgrounds in London and Kent.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Work outside London and Kent. Projects not focused on employment outcomes. Support for people over 30.
opportunities.application_process: Apply through the Skinners' Company website. Rolling applications. Provide evidence of need, delivery plan and expected employment outcomes.

opportunities.name: Asda Foundation - Local Community Spaces Fund
opportunities.description: Funding to support safe and welcoming community spaces that bring people together, connect communities and address loneliness and isolation. Open to groups throughout the UK providing spaces where people can meet.
opportunities.funder_priorities: Asda Foundation cares about creating safe, welcoming community spaces that bring people together. They prioritize tackling loneliness and isolation, connecting communities and creating places where everyone feels welcome. Community infrastructure is their focus.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Improvements to community buildings, creation of welcoming spaces, equipment for community venues, facilities that enable people to connect and reduce isolation.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Projects without a physical community space element. Individual support. Projects not addressing loneliness or isolation.
opportunities.application_process: Application deadline 28 April 2026. Apply through Asda Foundation website. Demonstrate how space brings community together and addresses isolation.

opportunities.name: AB Charitable Trust - Open Programme
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants for registered charities promoting human dignity and defending human rights of marginalised and excluded people. Focus on Human Rights Framework, Access to Justice, Criminal Legal System and Penal Reform, and Migrants and Refugees.
opportunities.amount: £10,000 - £30,000 per year for up to 3 years
opportunities.funder_priorities: AB Charitable Trust is passionate about promoting human dignity and defending human rights of marginalized and excluded people. They use language around justice, dignity, rights frameworks, and supporting those facing discrimination and exclusion.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted core funding for registered charities with income £150k-£1.5m working on human rights, access to justice, criminal legal system reform, migrants and refugees. Multi-year support.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Charities outside the income band. Projects not focused on human rights and marginalized groups. Capital projects.
opportunities.application_process: Deadline Friday 24 April 2026. Online application through AB Charitable Trust website. Requires accounts, governance documents, and clear human rights focus.

opportunities.name: King Charles III Charitable Fund - Small Grants Programme
opportunities.description: Supporting UK-registered nonprofit organisations working in environment, countryside, education, heritage and conservation, social inclusion and health and wellbeing. This round focuses on Environment and Countryside themes.
opportunities.amount: Up to £3,000 per year for up to 3 years
opportunities.funder_priorities: King Charles III Charitable Fund prioritizes supporting disadvantaged communities both urban and rural. They care about environmental protection, countryside access, heritage conservation and community wellbeing. Emphasis on reaching those with greatest need.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Small nonprofit organizations (income £25k-£500k) working on environment and countryside projects this round. Projects under £50k total cost. Organisations with less than 6 months reserves. Multi-year funding possible.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Food waste/food poverty (separate fund for this). Capital projects for heritage buildings. Organizations with income outside range. Those with more than 6 months reserves.
opportunities.application_process: Applications open 23 April - 7 May 2026 at 12 noon. Complete eligibility quiz first to receive unique application link. Priority given to most disadvantaged areas using Index of Multiple Deprivation.

opportunities.name: The Fore - Unrestricted Growth Grants
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants for small charities and CICs to strengthen organizations internally and help them take the next step in growth or sustainability. Not for ongoing operations but for capacity building, increasing sustainability or efficiency.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Fore prioritizes small charities and CICs that need to strengthen internally and grow. They care about organizational sustainability, capacity building, internal systems, and helping organizations become more efficient and resilient. Growth-oriented language.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted funding for capacity building, organizational development, new systems, serving new beneficiary groups, increasing sustainability or efficiency. Charities or CICs with income under £500k last completed year.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Ongoing operational costs. Projects without clear organizational development element. Organizations over income threshold.
opportunities.application_process: Registration window 8-15 July 2026 at 12pm. Two-stage process. Must demonstrate how grant strengthens organization internally. Includes non-financial support alongside grant.

opportunities.name: Ernest Kleinwort Charitable Trust - Medium Grants (Sussex)
opportunities.description: Supporting youth and community projects in Sussex, prioritizing vulnerable young people especially in deprived areas. Funding can cover core costs, new projects or unrestricted activities aligned with Trust priorities.
opportunities.funder_priorities: Ernest Kleinwort Charitable Trust focuses on supporting vulnerable young people in Sussex, particularly in deprived areas. They prioritize youth welfare, community support, care of elderly, disability support, hospices, and wildlife & environmental conservation in Sussex.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Registered charities in England, Wales or Scotland with at least one year of accounts. Youth and community projects in Sussex. Core costs including operational and staffing. Unrestricted funding if aligned with priorities.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations without at least one year of filed accounts. Work outside Sussex (for this grant). National charities without distinct local service.
opportunities.application_process: Applications open 18 April - 12 May 2026. Online application. Requires accounts, evidence of work with young people or community in Sussex, and clear project plan.

opportunities.name: Schroder Charity Trust - Small Grants
opportunities.description: Two focused objectives: enabling children and young adults from disadvantaged backgrounds to thrive in education and employment; and strengthening communities through services for vulnerable and disadvantaged people. Two application windows per year.
opportunities.funder_priorities: Schroder Charity Trust focuses on enabling children and young adults from disadvantaged backgrounds to achieve their potential in education and employment. They also care about strengthening communities and improving wellbeing and life outcomes for vulnerable and disadvantaged people.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Projects supporting disadvantaged children and young adults in education and employment. Community services enhancing wellbeing and life outcomes for vulnerable people. Training, skills development, employability programmes.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Large established charities with substantial reserves. Projects outside their two objectives. Individual support.
opportunities.application_process: Current window closes 30 April 2026. Two application windows per year. Online application through Schroder Charity Trust website. Provide clear outcomes focused on education/employment or community wellbeing.
